editorial_note.summary: Synthetic MCHK inquiry in which inadequate clinical recordkeeping was admitted and found proved, resulting in a warning letter and no costs order.
editorial_note.takeaway: For demo purposes, an admitted recordkeeping charge can still attract a formal warning even with a clear prior record; exact sanction wording remains in the published proposition claims.

editorial_note.summary: Synthetic DCHK inquiry concerning unsuitable dental premises/equipment; the charge was proved and a short removal order was suspended.
editorial_note.takeaway: The demo illustrates premises fitness as a distinct issue class and a suspended removal as the normalised sanction category, separate from remorse as a mitigating factor.

editorial_note.summary: Minimal synthetic MCHK PDF fixture: inadequate records charge proved with a warning letter order.
editorial_note.takeaway: Used to exercise PDF segmentation and public excerpt publication; classifications mirror the short fixture text only.
